# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: Continuous Deployment (CI)
on: [push] # or 'pull_request' if you want that event triggering manually and not automatically on each push ✨💡️
jobs:      
    deploy-production:  
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest         
        
        steps:                       
            - name: Checkout Code             # step for checking out the repository to runner     
              uses: actions/checkout@v2    
              
           # Step 1 of a multi job run      
           ./.github.workflows/*install-deps*/run  
                 
          /* Execute all commands here, such as running npm install and building production artifact */   
            - name: Run Tests              # step for the test to fail if it fails or change ‘fail’ status qubit      ✅        👌️     
             run : echo "No tests defined in this workflow."     # replace with your commands.   'npm install && npm ci' can be an example of what command you want for testing   
             
           - name: Deploy to Production  # step will fail if it fails or change the failure status qubit       🏎️        ✅         ^               (use escape sequences like \r\n below lines, and have a look at examples)   &       *******                
             run : |                   echo "No steps to be executed when deploying in production. Please check your workflow."    return     ./*deploy-prod*              # change this line as per deployment commands (e.g., 'npm publish')  or use escape sequences like \r\n below lines, and have a look at examples
